tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: A Teenager's Perfect Father's Day Gift: Playing Catch With Dad at the U.S. Open
stories.summary: Miles Russell, 17, surprises his dad Joe by handing him the caddie bib for the final hole at his first U.S. Open, creating an unforgettable Father's Day moment.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Tiny Curacao Earns Historic First World Cup Point
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Eloy Room, 37, makes a record 15 saves to help tiny Curacao earn their first-ever World Cup point with a 0-0 draw against Ecuador.

stories.headline: 'Have Faith': A Mother's Journey Across the World to Cheer Her Son at the World Cup
stories.summary: Cape Verde's goalkeeper Vozinha's mother finally arrives to watch her son play at the World Cup after overcoming visa struggles.

stories.headline: Neighbors' Kindness Overflows: Rescue Dog Gets So Many Gifts They Return Extras to the Shelter
stories.summary: A rescue dog owner receives so many donated beds and supplies from neighbors that they return half to the shelter to help other animals.

stories.headline: Teen's Eagle Scout Project: 150+ Suitcases So Foster Kids Travel With Dignity
stories.summary: Grayson Herr, a Chicago high school senior, collects over 150 suitcases for foster children so they can travel with dignity between homes.

stories.headline: College Students Open Free Thrift Store, Welcome 600 Neighbors on Day One
stories.summary: Young entrepreneurs launch a dignity-centered free shop where 600 neighbors discover 2,515 items of hope and possibility on opening day.

stories.headline: Three Chicks Hatch at Blackpool Zoo, Offering Hope for World's Most Endangered Songbirds
stories.summary: Three blue-crowned laughingthrush chicks hatch at Blackpool Zoo, offering hope for a species with only 50 to 249 birds left in the wild.

stories.headline: 'Pure Compassion': One Man's Nightly Mission to Feed Stray Cows Touches Thousands
stories.summary: A devoted volunteer goes door-to-door each night collecting rotis to feed hungry stray cows, touching thousands with his quiet compassion.

stories.headline: Two Rescued Sea Turtles Begin Their Journey Home to the Reef
stories.summary: Squirdle and Treasure, two loggerhead turtles rescued during 2024 winter storms, fly 1,400 kilometres from Bunbury to be released near Ningaloo Reef after two years of care.

stories.headline: From Doubt to Glory: Wyndham Clark Wins Second U.S. Open After Overcoming Personal Storms
stories.summary: Wyndham Clark wins his second U.S. Open three years after his first, proving redemption through personal struggles and public criticism.

stories.headline: A Father's Day Gift: Four Thriving Piping Plover Chicks Turn One Week Old
stories.summary: Chicago's piping plover Imani's four chicks reach one week old, watched by 115 devoted community monitors celebrating new life.

stories.headline: Father's Day on the Diamond: A Team and Their Fathers Rally Around Grief and Hope
stories.summary: North Carolina baseball players and their fathers gather on Father's Day to support Erik Paulsen Jr. in his first without his late father, as the team forces a title-deciding game.

stories.headline: Pure Hydrogen Powers a Modern Jet Engine—Clean Flight Is Now Real
stories.summary: Rolls-Royce achieves a breakthrough: a modern jet engine runs on 100% hydrogen at full takeoff power, making clean flight engineering reality.

stories.headline: Lytton Rises Again: A Community's Open Heart Through a Second Wildfire
stories.summary: Lytton opens its doors again through a second wildfire, with motel owners sheltering firefighters and neighbors welcoming evacuees with open hearts.

stories.headline: World's First AI-Designed Vaccine Shows Promise in Human Trial
stories.summary: Researchers at Cambridge test the first AI-designed vaccine in 39 human volunteers, generating immune responses against multiple coronavirus strains safely.

stories.headline: The First 'Reverse-Ageing' Trial Begins: Can We Restore Sight by Turning Back Time?
stories.summary: Boston biotech Life Biosciences doses the first patient in a trial of ER-100, the first cellular reprogramming therapy to rejuvenate damaged eye cells in humans.

stories.headline: Marizanne Kapp's Blazing Bat Powers South Africa Into World Cup Contention
stories.summary: Marizanne Kapp blazes an unbeaten 81 off 45 balls to power South Africa past India and keep their World Cup playoff hopes alive.
